data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Update: Futures Rise Late Wednesday Ahead of Inflation Data
data.description:
U.S. stock futures rose late Wednesday as global tensions eased, helping markets rebound after a sharp selloff earlier in the week. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were up 0.46%, 0.18%, and 0.31%, respectively, at 8:41 p.m. EDT on January 21.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Earlier in the session, stocks jumped sharply after President Donald Trump said he would not move forward with new tariffs on Europe that were set to begin on Feb. 1. He also announced a "framework" deal related to Greenland, which helped calm investor concerns.

Looking ahead, investors are now turning their attention to Thursday morning. Markets are waiting for the release of the personal consumption expenditures (PCE) price index, a key inflation measure.

In addition, earnings remain in focus. Procter & Gamble (PG), Intel (INTC), and GE Aerospace (GE) are scheduled to report quarterly results on Thursday, which could further influence market sentiment.

Stay ahead of macro events with our up-to-the-minute Economic Calendar — filter by impact, country, and more.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Why Lululemon Founder Calls Leggings Launch a Total Failure
data.description:
Lululemon Athletica (LULU) is facing renewed pressure after its founder Chip Wilson publicly criticized the company over a failed product launch involving see-through leggings. Wilson called the issue a "total operational failure" and placed the blame on Lululemon's board of directors. His comments followed customer complaints that the company's new "Get Low" leggings were see-through.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Wilson said the leggings launch marked a "new low for Lululemon," arguing that repeated missteps reflect deeper problems at the board level. In his view, the board lacks experience in creative businesses and has placed too little emphasis on product quality.

Lululemon Pauses Online Sales

Lululemon paused online sales of the Get Low leggings just days after the company launched on its website. The product remains available in physical stores.

The company said it stopped e-commerce sales "to better understand some initial guest feedback and support with product education," adding that it expects to resume online sales soon. Lululemon did not respond to requests for comment on Wilson's remarks.

The setback adds to Lululemon's recent struggles as it works to regain sales momentum. About 18 months ago, the company pulled its Breezethrough line shortly after launch due to customer complaints about fit.

Leadership and Investor Pressure Builds

Wilson remains one of Lululemon's largest shareholders and has been openly pushing for changes at the company he founded in 1998. He has repeatedly called for a board shakeup.

Last month, Lululemon announced that Chief Executive Officer Calvin McDonald will step down at the end of January, and the board has begun searching for a new CEO.

At the same time, activist investor Elliott Investment Management has built a stake of more than $1 billion in Lululemon. Elliott has been working with retail veteran Jane Nielsen, a former senior executive at Ralph Lauren, as it pushes for changes at the apparel maker.

Is LULU Stock a Buy?

The stock of Lululemon Athletica has a consensus Hold rating among 22 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on one Buy and 21 Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average LULU price target of $208.19 implies 9.52% upside from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Rocket Lab Stock (RKLB) Falls on Neutron Testing Update
data.description:
Rocket Lab (RKLB) stock declined 4.5% in Wednesday's extended trading session after the space company and launch services provider issued an update about the testing of its Neutron rocket. The company disclosed that during a hydrostatic pressure trial, the overnight qualification testing of the Stage 1 tank resulted in a rupture.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Rocket Lab Offers Update on Neutron Rocket

Rocket Lab stated that it is reviewing the Stage 1 test data and will decide the extent of the impact on Neutron's launch schedule following the review. It said that there was no notable damage to the test structure or facilities. In fact, RKLB highlighted that the next Stage 1 tank is already in production.

"Testing failures are not uncommon during qualification testing," stated the company.

The company added that it "intentionally" tests structures to their limits to ensure structural integrity and safety margins, given the significant requirements for a successful launch. Rocket Lab will provide an update on Neutron's schedule during its Q4 2025 earnings call in February.

Rocket Lab's Neutron rocket is viewed as a key growth catalyst. In November 2025, the company disappointed investors when it disclosed during its Q3 2025 earnings call that the Neutron launch was delayed from late 2025 to 2026. Nevertheless, RKLB stock has rallied 181% over the past year, driven by the optimism about space technology companies and the ongoing geopolitical tensions.

Is RKLB Stock a Good Buy?

Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Rocket Lab stock based on seven Buys and four Holds. The average RKLB stock price target of $82.44 indicates a 6% downside risk from current levels.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Nvidia's (NVDA) Jensen Huang Says Europe Has a "Once-in-a-Generation" Chance to Lead in Robotics
data.description:
At the World Economic Forum in Davos, Nvidia (NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang said Europe has a rare, "once‑in‑a‑generation" opportunity to lead in robotics.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Huang said Europe's long history in industrial manufacturing could become a major strength in the new robotics era. By pairing its engineering and production skills with modern AI, Europe can skip the software-focused phase and move straight into what he calls "physical AI," or AI that powers real-world machines and automation.

He pointed to major European companies, Siemens (SIEGY), Mercedes-Benz (MBGAF), Volvo (VLVLY), and Schaeffler, that are already investing heavily in robotics and AI-driven manufacturing.

Global Race Toward Robotics

Huang also noted that U.S. tech giants are investing significant resources in robotics. Tesla's (TSLA) Elon Musk has said that 80% of the company's future value could come from its Optimus humanoid robots. Also, Google's (GOOGL) DeepMind has released advanced AI models for robotics, and Nvidia itself is partnering with Alphabet to accelerate the development of "physical AI."

Huang argued that this global momentum makes it even more urgent for Europe to act quickly.

He also warned that Europe faces a big challenge, high energy costs, and a limited power supply. Moreover, Huang noted that without a stronger energy system, Europe will have a hard time building the AI infrastructure to support large-scale robotics.

Energy Constraints Could Slow Europe's Robotics Ambitions

Despite the opportunity, Huang warned that Europe faces a big challenge: high energy costs and limited power supply. Without a stronger energy system, he said, Europe will have a hard time building the AI infrastructure needed to support large-scale robotics.

He urged policymakers to "get serious" about boosting energy capacity so the region can support data centers, AI factories, and the wider ecosystem needed for robotics innovation.

Is Nvidia a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Nvidia stock based on 39 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell recommendation. The average NVDA stock price target of $263.44 indicates 43.7% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Why Did BigBear.ai Stock (BBAI) Fall 4% Yesterday?
data.description:
Shares of BigBear.ai Holdings (BBAI) fell nearly 4% on January 21 after Pomerantz LLP, a prominent securities law firm, announced an investigation of potential securities fraud. The "investor alert" triggered a sell-off, underscoring the company's vulnerability to fraud-related news following a major setback last year. In March 2025, BigBear.ai delayed its annual report filing and restated historical results through 2022 due to incorrect accounting treatment of convertible debt.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

On a positive note, BigBear.ai announced the acquisition of certain technologies of CargoSeer, adding AI-powered inspection and trade-risk management tools. This move could broaden its commercial and supply-chain offerings while strengthening recurring revenue potential.

BigBear.ai Faces Accounting Scrutiny Again

Pomerantz LLP has urged BBAI shareholders to contact the firm regarding potential claims. Plaintiff attorneys typically issue such "investor alerts" as a standard step, warning shareholders about possible securities fraud by a company or its management.

These announcements can lead to heavy selling pressure due to fears of accounting misconduct and shareholder losses, even though an investigation does not equate to a lawsuit or proven wrongdoing.

Analyst Downgrade Adds Pressure

In the notice, Pomerantz also referenced BBAI's recent stock downgrade to "Hold" from "Buy" by top Cantor Fitzgerald analyst Jonathan Ruykhaver. He cut the price target from $7 to $6, implying 5.1% upside potential from current levels.

Ruykhaver noted that near-term fundamentals stay under pressure, as Q3 revenue fell 20.1% year-over-year due to federal program disruptions. He highlighted ongoing execution risks from BigBear.ai's reliance on irregular government contracts, which continue to fuel operating losses and margin strain.

Does BBAI Have a Future?

Analysts remain divided about BBAI's long-term outlook. On TipRanks, BBAI has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on one Buy and two Hold ratings. The average BigBear.ai price target of $6.33 implies nearly 11% upside potential from current levels. Despite the ongoing issues, BBAI shares have gained 28.6% over the past year.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: BitGo's $212.8M IPO Puts Crypto Stocks Back on Investors' Radar
data.description:
Crypto stocks may be edging back into the spotlight. U.S.-based BitGo priced its IPO above expectations on Wednesday, raising $212.8 million. The offering marks the first public listing by a digital asset company in 2026, despite ongoing regulatory hurdles and volatility in the crypto market. BitGo's debut serves as an early test of investor demand. While the IPO signals renewed interest in crypto stocks, a full comeback will likely require steadier markets and clearer regulations.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For context, BitGo is a crypto custody and security firm that helps institutions store, manage, and protect digital assets. It provides regulated infrastructure for trading, staking, and settlement across the crypto market.

More Details on BitGo IPO

BitGo's shares will trade on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) on Thursday under the ticker BTGO. The company sold 11.8 million shares at $18 each, topping its earlier price range of $15 to $17 per share.

Overall, the offering values BitGo at about $2.08 billion.

Tough Times for Crypto Stocks

Interestingly, the IPO comes at a challenging time for the U.S. crypto industry. Lawmakers are moving forward with a long-awaited market structure bill that could reshape how crypto is regulated.

Additionally, the sector has also been rattled by a sharp crypto selloff in October, making investors more cautious and raising the bar for companies trying to raise money. Reports say crypto exchange Kraken is among the firms considering IPOs this year.

BitGo is the first crypto company to move ahead with a U.S. IPO in 2026, after several digital asset firms went public last year. For instance, Circle (CRCL) and Figure (FIGR) went public earlier in 2025 during a much stronger period for crypto stocks. That more upbeat market helped both companies post sharp gains on their first day of trading.

Which Is the Best Crypto Stock to Buy, According to Analysts?

Investors interested in the crypto sector can look at leading publicly traded crypto-related stocks. Tools like TipRanks' Best Cryptocurrency Stocks can help investors research potential opportunities using analyst ratings and insights.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Baidu Stock (BIDU) Rallies to 3-Year High on Ernie 5.0 Launch – Outperforms Google, OpenAI
data.description:
Shares of Chinese tech giant Baidu (BIDU) (HK:9888) rallied nearly 10% to a near three-year high on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange following the official release of its Ernie 5.0 AI model. Baidu claims Ernie 5.0 surpasses leading global AI models like Alphabet (GOOGL) Google's Gemini 2.5 Pro and OpenAI's GPT-5, especially in reasoning, comprehension, and handling complex tasks.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The shares hit a new 52-week high of HK$160.90, extending the prior day's 8.2% surge on the Nasdaq. On January 20, Baidu announced that its AI assistant, powered by its Ernie large language model (LLM), has now surpassed 200 million monthly active users (MAUs), suggesting rapid adoption of its generative AI ecosystem. Additionally, its Apollo Go robotaxi service officially began commercial operations in Abu Dhabi, marking a big step in Baidu's global expansion of autonomous driving technology.

Baidu Cloud Is the Core Driver of Long-Term Growth

Baidu launched the Wenxin Big Model 5.0, aka Ernie 5.0, at an AI-focused conference in Shanghai. The model is now available on Baidu's cloud platform and represents a major advance in multimodal AI. It uses native full-modal modeling to seamlessly process text, images, audio, and video. With a massive 2.4 trillion parameters, it ranks among the most powerful LLMs on the market today.

The release sparked fresh investor optimism, underscoring Baidu's rising dominance in China's booming AI market and its bold push to rival global leaders. Baidu first unveiled the model in November 2025, drawing a similar positive response.

Over the past two years, Baidu has ramped up AI investments, targeting foundational models, cloud services, and practical enterprise uses. Ernie 5.0's launch cements the company's leadership in China's AI sector, while establishing it as a formidable global player in cutting-edge AI tech.

Is Baidu Stock a Buy Now?

Analysts remain highly optimistic about Baidu's long-term prospects. On TipRanks, BIDU shares have a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 10 Buys and two Hold ratings. The average Baidu price target of $164.81 implies 1.6% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, BIDU shares have surged nearly 96%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Gold Headed Higher? Goldman Sachs (GS) Lifts Year-End Forecast to $5,400 an Ounce
data.description:
Wall Street investment bank Goldman Sachs (GS) raised its year-end gold price forecast by more than 10%, pointing to steady demand from private investors on top of strong buying by central banks and ETFs. The bank lifted its December 2026 gold target to $5,400 an ounce, up from a prior call of $4,900, according to a note dated January 21.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Goldman said the higher forecast assumes that private investors who bought gold as a hedge against policy risks will hold on to those positions through the end of the year.

Why Goldman Is More Bullish on Gold

Goldman said today's gold buying is not tied to one-off events like elections. Instead, investors are using gold to guard against longer-term risks, such as high debt levels and unclear policy direction. Because these risks are unlikely to ease soon, the bank expects investors to hold their gold positions for longer.

Gold prices have already jumped more than 70% over the past year and continue to hit new highs in early 2026. The rise reflects strong demand for safe assets as global tensions grow and concerns increase about the independence of the U.S. Federal Reserve.

Goldman also sees strong support from central banks, which are expected to buy about 60 tons of gold per month in 2026, mainly from emerging markets. In addition, Western gold ETFs have added roughly 500 tons since early 2025. Meanwhile, lower interest rates could add further support, as Goldman expects the Fed to cut rates by another 50 basis points in 2026.

Goldman is not the only firm. Other Wall Street banks also see gold prices rising through 2026 amid ongoing global uncertainty.

Is GS Stock a Buy?

Goldman stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 11 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 5 Buy and six Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average GS price target of $947.60 implies 0.57% downside from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: NVDA, PLTR: Daniel Ives Hails Nvidia and Palantir as AI Powerhouses at Davos
data.description:
Wedbush's top analyst, Daniel Ives, shared insights from the World Economic Forum in Davos, where he met with global tech executives. He noted that the AI revolution remained the key theme, despite the ongoing geopolitical tensions involving Greenland. Ives was particularly impressed that Nvidia (NVDA) and Palantir Technologies (PLTR) stood out as prime beneficiaries of what he calls the "fourth industrial revolution."

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ives emphasized that talks centered on AI investment, adoption, and monetization. He asserted, "The one thing that is clear from Davos is the U.S. tech world is dominating the AI Revolution with China a distant second."

Ives is a five-star analyst on TipRanks, ranking #390 out of 12,040 analysts tracked. He has a 56% success rate and an average return per rating of 16.80%.

Nvidia Is the Central Force Behind AI

Ives noted that Nvidia remains the central force powering global AI development. He expects soaring demand from AI hyperscalers such as Microsoft (MSFT), Amazon (AMZN), and Alphabet (GOOGL) to fuel the next growth phase, as Nvidia's GPUs (graphics processing units) power their AI tech and software.

Despite investor worries about high valuations and the massive capital investments required to sustain AI, Ives believes the market is understating the opportunity. He projects about $3 trillion in AI-related outlays from enterprises and governments over the next three years, with more than $550 billion already underway.

Wedbush has a Buy rating on NVDA and a $230 price target, implying 25.5% upside potential.

Palantir Is a Clear Star in the AI Revolution

Ives stated that Palantir stands out as one of the largest beneficiaries of the AI revolution. As companies shift from AI hype to real-world results, Palantir's Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP) helps translate investments into measurable revenue.

Ives positions Palantir as a frontrunner in the AI monetization era, stemming from its ability to deliver actionable AI solutions that align with enterprise needs. Its actionable solutions set it apart, enabling chief information officers (CIOs) across sectors to turn strategic plans into business gains.

Ives has a Buy rating and a $230 price target on PLTR, suggesting 39.1% upside potential.

Big Tech Earnings to Validate the Boom

Ives anticipates the Q4 earnings season will validate the AI investment boom, led by cloud and AI giants. He noted that Wedbush's field research shows strong enterprise AI demand for Microsoft, Alphabet, and Amazon during the quarter. Ives expects capex plans will ramp up even more in 2026.

Ending Thoughts

Ives compared the current moment to mid-1996 rather than the overheated 1999 bubble, noting that AI-driven growth remains in its infancy and is yet to be priced into tech valuations. Looking ahead, Ives predicts that expanding real-world AI applications in software and semiconductors will dominate the next 12 to 18 months, bolstering Wedbush's bullish outlook as the AI revolution accelerates.

Using the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool we observe that NVDA stock has a "Strong Buy" consensus and a 43.7% upside potential over the next twelve months, signaling solid analyst conviction in the stock. Meanwhile, Wall Street remains sidelined on PLTR after its 134% stock price surge over the past year.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Cathie Wood Buys Netflix and AI Stocks, Dumps Beam, PINS, and GitLab Shares
data.description:
Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs (exchange-traded funds) disclosed their daily ETF trades for Wednesday, January 21, 2026. The firm added to streaming giant Netflix (NFLX) and several AI stocks, while trimming exposure to select biotech and software stocks.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Which Stocks Did Wood Buy on January 21?

1. Netflix Stock – Wood made her largest buy of the day in Netflix, purchasing 83,368 shares through the ARK Next Generation Internet ETF (ARKW) for about $7.27 million, signaling confidence in Netflix's long-term growth. Netflix shares fell 2% yesterday even after reporting better-than-expected Q4 results as investors remain more focused on Netflix's proposed all-cash deal for Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) than the quarter itself.  Netflix posted fourth-quarter revenue of $12.1 billion, modestly above Wall Street expectations of $11.97 billion, while adjusted earnings came in at $0.56 per share, also slightly ahead of forecasts of $0.55 per share.

    2. Tempus AI Stock (TEM) – Wood bought 89,501 shares through the ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK) and the ARK Genomic Revolution ETF (ARKG), investing about $5.98 million to expand her AI-driven healthcare exposure.  TEM shares dipped 2.2% on January 21. The move comes as AI tools see wider use in health care data work.

    3. WeRide Stock (WRD) – Wood bought 111,439 shares of WeRide through the ARK Autonomous Technology & Robotics ETF (ARKQ), worth about $945,000. The buy follows earlier purchases as self-driving tests and pilot programs continue to expand. WRD shares rose 4.3% on January 21.

    4. Trimble (TRMB) – Wood bought 8,817 shares of Trimble through the ARK Space Exploration & Innovation ETF (ARKX) for about $614,000. The purchase comes as demand remains steady for industrial software and positioning tools used in construction and aerospace projects.

    To compare how these newly added positions stack up, let's see how these stocks perform using the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool.
